applicant_information.name_title_phone_p.answer: Maria Sanchez – Line Producer / On-Site Production Manager – Mobile: (505) 555-0193

location_information.location_of_site_de.answer: BLM-managed lands in central New Mexico, approximately 30–50 miles west of Albuquerque, consisting of open high-desert landscape with low rolling hills, sparse juniper, and exposed rock formations suitable for portraying a remote frontier setting.

location_information.specific_locations_.answer:
Primary: A 2–3 mile stretch of unpaved BLM access road and adjacent open desert flats off County Road 279 near the Rio Puerco area, including a low ridgeline with panoramic views to the west for sunrise and sunset shots.
Secondary: A dry arroyo and surrounding scrubland approximately 0.5 miles south of the primary road segment for walk-and-talk scenes and camp set dressing. All exact GPS coordinates to be finalized in coordination with the local BLM field office; all activity will remain within pre-approved pullouts, existing disturbed areas, and designated access routes to avoid sensitive habitats and cultural resources.

type_and_purpose_of_use.state_name_of_fe.answer: Sunridge Outfitters – "End of the Road" Fall 2026 Campaign (Television & Online Commercial Spots)

use_schedule_and_personnel.dates_of_use.answer: 2026-10-14 to 2026-10-16 (three consecutive shoot days on BLM land; 2026-10-13 and 2026-10-17 reserved as travel and weather backup days with no on-land filming unless separately approved).

use_schedule_and_personnel.hours_of_use.answer:
Daily crew call on BLM land: 06:00–18:30.
Shooting hours: Approximately 07:00–11:30 and 14:30–18:00, with a midday break for lunch, equipment checks, and quiet time. Earliest arrival of any vehicle on site will not be before 05:45, and all vehicles and personnel will depart the site no later than 19:00 each day. Night filming is not planned; no artificial lighting beyond portable, battery-powered LED panels and small reflectors will be used.

activity_and_animals.statement_of_activi.answer:
The requested activity is a three-day commercial video shoot for Sunridge Outfitters, an outdoor apparel and gear company. The creative concept follows a small, diverse group of travelers on a road trip through the high desert at the symbolic 'end of the road,' emphasizing resilience, connection to landscape, and responsible recreation on public lands.

PLANNED SCENES AND ACTIONS:
1. ROAD DRIVING SEQUENCES: Two picture vehicles (a late-model dark gray 4x4 pickup and a silver crossover SUV) will be filmed driving at safe, legal speeds along an existing BLM dirt access road. All driving will remain on established roads and previously disturbed pullouts. There will be no off-road or high-speed stunt driving. We will film:
   - Wide establishing shots of vehicles approaching and cresting a low hill.
   - Medium tracking shots from a camera vehicle driving ahead of or behind the picture vehicles.
   - Static tripod shots from the shoulder or designated pullouts, with crew and gear kept well off the traveled way.

2. WALKING AND HIKING SEQUENCES: Four principal talent (two male-presenting, two female-presenting, ages mid-20s to mid-40s) will be filmed walking along existing social trails and dry washes, pausing to look at the horizon, check maps, and interact with each other. No new trails will be created; talent will be instructed to stay on already-disturbed surfaces. Scenes will show:
   - Characters donning jackets, backpacks, and hats (Sunridge products).
   - Characters consulting a paper map and GPS device, emphasizing navigation and safety.
   - Slow-motion shots of boots on sandy soil and gravel, captured from low-angle camera positions.

3. CAMP-STYLE SETUP (DAY USE ONLY): A minimal, day-use camp vignette will be staged in a pre-approved disturbed area near the vehicles. This will NOT be an actual overnight camp. We will set up:
   - Two camp chairs and a small folding table.
   - A closed, unlit camp stove (for visual purposes only; no open flame or cooking unless specifically authorized, and we are currently planning for no live fire given fall conditions and fire restrictions).
   - A cooler and small props (coffee mugs, notebooks, binoculars). All props will be removed daily.
   - Talent will simulate pouring drinks (using water or cooled herbal tea) and eating pre-packaged snacks. All trash will be packed out.

4. DIALOGUE AND CLOSE-UP SHOTS: Short scripted lines will be recorded with handheld or boom microphones. Content focuses on themes of perseverance, friendship, and respect for the land. Example dialogue includes comments about staying on designated routes, packing out trash, and checking weather conditions. Close-ups will show product details (zippers, seams, fabric texture) and talent expressions against the desert backdrop.

5. DRONE USE (IF APPROVED BY BLM AND FAA): We intend to apply separately for permission to operate a small, registered quadcopter drone (sub-55 lbs) for limited aerial establishing shots, flown by an FAA Part 107 certified remote pilot. Drone flights will:
   - Occur only during daylight, in good weather, and below 400 feet AGL.
   - Avoid wildlife, livestock, and other visitors; flights will be suspended immediately if raptors or other sensitive species are observed nearby.
   - Be limited in total duration (no more than 90 minutes of flight time across all days).
If drone use is not approved, these scenes will be adapted to ground-based wide shots.

6. SOUND AND POWER: We will primarily use battery-powered equipment. A single small, quiet portable generator (approx. 2,000W inverter type) may be used for battery charging and laptop backups, placed on an existing disturbed surface away from sensitive vegetation and muffled to minimize noise. No amplified music or PA system will be used. Sound levels will be kept low, and we will pause recording if other visitors are present to avoid disruption.

7. CREW MOVEMENT AND ACCESS: All cast and crew will arrive in a maximum of 10 production vehicles (mix of vans, SUVs, and a small grip truck), parking only in existing pullouts or areas designated by BLM staff. We will:
   - Conduct a safety and environmental briefing each morning emphasizing staying on roads and trails, respecting cultural resources, and proper waste disposal.
   - Use existing two-track or well-compacted areas for any equipment staging.
   - Avoid climbing on rock formations, disturbing biological soil crusts, or moving rocks, vegetation, or cultural artifacts. If any artifacts are discovered, activity in that area will stop and BLM will be notified immediately.

8. SAFETY AND MEDICAL: We will have a dedicated set medic (EMT-B certified) on site with a well-stocked first aid kit, AED, and cooling/heating packs. The medic will maintain a log of any incidents, and all crew will be briefed on:
   - Hydration (we will bring at least 1.5 gallons of water per person per day).
   - Sun protection (hats, sunscreen, long sleeves).
   - Wildlife awareness (snakes, insects) and procedures for non-harassment and safe distance.
A detailed safety plan, including nearest hospital (Presbyterian Hospital in Albuquerque) and evacuation routes, will be provided to BLM upon request.

9. CLEANUP AND RESTORATION: At the end of each day, the production assistant team will perform a line sweep of all used areas to ensure removal of all trash, tape, and any temporary markers. We will not dig, stake, or otherwise disturb the ground surface without explicit prior approval. Temporary markers, if needed, will be removable flags or cones, not paint or permanent indicators. Our goal is to leave the site in equal or better condition than we found it.

Overall, the activities are low-impact, daytime-only commercial filming operations focused on small groups of people, light vehicles on existing roads, and portable camera equipment. No pyrotechnics, weapons, simulated combat, loud special effects, or large constructed sets will be used.
